repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1: joint offline-policy misspecification with an outcome-mean tube, though not this repaired coordinatewise MSM truncation formula

published_prior_art.cite: YadlowskyNamkoongBasuDuchiTian2022 and ZhangXuTan2025 give mean-side MSM tightening templates, not the offline-policy mean-versus-quantile dichotomy

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1 and failed pid_qte_marginal_sensitivity_v1 flag joint compatibility and quantile-pasting issues, but do not state this fixed-center offline-policy conditional-quantile-tube endpoint

published_prior_art.cite: HessFrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2026 gives MSM-only sharp off-policy value; DornGuo2023 gives bounded-weight quantile geometry; neither imposes a fixed-center quantile-process tube on the same latent law

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_offline_policy_joint_misspec_v1: non-rectangular diagnostic for joint misspecification, but not the proposed quantile-envelope representability criterion

published_prior_art.cite: HessFrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2026 and ZhangXuTan2025 do not state a Delta_rect equality/strictness criterion for pointwise rectangular quantile envelopes

HessFrauenMelnychukFeuerriegel2026 already supplies the MSM-only score and one-step estimator; Firpo2007 and ChernozhukovEtAl2018 supply the inverse-CDF and DML templates, but not the active tube-contact correction

novelty_flags.label: proposal handoff / Hess-Frauen comparator positioning
novelty_flags.one_line: The prose sells a correction to the Hess/Frauen sharp off-policy workflow, but no statement is phrased as a strict tightening, iff frontier, or non-representability result against their published endpoint or estimator; the required comparator-promise table is also missing.

novelty_flags.label: related_work / joint distributional MSM comparators
novelty_flags.one_line: The survey misses Zhang-Xu-Tan's deMSM arXiv:2505.13868, which adds potential-outcome distribution-shift constraints to MSM and is closer to a law-level tube than the cited eMSM mean-bound paper.

soundness_flags.label: def:qcpl-plus / def:qcpl-minus / thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint
soundness_flags.one_line: The claimed model is the symmetric quantile tube sup_u |Q-Q0| <= epsilon, but the upper optimizer imposes only q <= Q0+epsilon and the lower optimizer only q >= Q0-epsilon, so the stated endpoints are for a different one-sided restriction.

soundness_flags.label: thm:mean-decoupling
soundness_flags.one_line: The mean-tube theorem lacks a nonempty-intersection condition between [M_a_minus,M_a_plus] and [m0_a-epsilon,m0_a+epsilon], so the displayed min/max endpoints can describe an empty armwise class.

soundness_flags.label: conj:eif-one-step / A_a_plus
soundness_flags.one_line: The EIF correction depends on the active-contact set of Qcpl_a_plus, but Qcpl_a_plus is defined as an argmax without a uniqueness or exposed-face convention, so phi_tube_plus is not yet a well-defined functional.

soundness_flags.label: ass:kink-margin
soundness_flags.one_line: The margin condition takes an infimum over the complement of the equality set K_a; under continuous quantile envelopes, any nonempty contact boundary makes that infimum zero, so the stated justification does not plausibly cover the advertised active-contact regimes.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: ass:joint-feasible / thm:quantile-coupled-endpoint
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The sharp endpoint is conditional on the MSM likelihood-ratio window and the fixed-center quantile tube having a nonempty intersection on policy-relevant arms.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The identification claim should be read as sharp conditional on joint feasibility, with incompatible laws yielding an empty restricted class rather than a finite endpoint.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Repair def:qcpl-plus and def:qcpl-minus so each optimizer enforces both sides of the fixed-center quantile tube, then restate the rectangular outer envelopes and Delta_rect criterion under the same symmetric class.
Add a primitive mean-feasibility assumption or an empty-set convention for Theorem 1, and state the endpoint as the intersection of the MSM mean interval with the additive mean tube.

Add Zhang-Xu-Tan deMSM as a load-bearing comparator and explain why its potential-outcome distribution-shift bounds do not subsume a fixed-center conditional quantile-process tube for offline policy value.

For the EIF conjecture, impose or derive uniqueness of the coupled optimizer, or redefine phi_tube_plus using an optimizer-invariant exposed face/subgradient so the active-contact functional is well-defined.

recommended_next_step: Continue revision on this angle; the kernel is field-plausible, but the quantile-tube endpoint definitions and comparator positioning need repair before Stage 0.
